summary: Large ftMasterHand decompilation PR that replaced most of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmasterhand.s with C split across src/melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/ftMasterHand_1.c through ftMasterHand_34.c plus a new ftMasterHand.h. It also updated fighter-wide headers/struct fields, function declarations, object file lists, and build tooling around frank.py. Review focused on organizing the files into an ftMasterHand folder, disabling a temporary Makefile map-generation/debug change before merge, simplifying a countdown condition, and using plain `return;` stubs instead of noisier empty-function bodies.
what_changed: Deleted approximately 7546 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmasterhand.s, leaving only the minimal assembly wrapper/include remnants for that file.

Added 34 C translation-unit slices under src/melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/, with the largest additions in ftMasterHand_2.c, ftMasterHand_22.c, ftMasterHand_15.c, ftMasterHand_30.c, and ftMasterHand_9.c.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/ftMasterHand.h with Master Hand-specific declarations/types used by the split C files.
Updated include/functions.h with many declaration changes around ftMasterHand and related engine/fighter functions.

Extended src/melee/ft/fighter.h with fields used by Master Hand code, including offsets visible in the diff excerpt such as x2344_callback, x2348_stateVar3, x234C_pos, x2358_stateVar7, x2360, x2364, and x2200_ftcmd_var0.
Updated obj_files.mk to build the new ftMasterHand C object files instead of relying on the monolithic assembly file.

Adjusted Makefile frank rules so the generated object target depends on $(FRANK) and passes explicit vanilla/profile object arguments using $(word 1,$^) and $(word 2,$^) instead of forwarding all prerequisites as $^.
Modified tools/frank.py, though the excerpt only establishes that frank tooling changed, not the detailed algorithm.
Made small related changes in src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c and src/melee/gr/ground.c.
smart_moves: Split the very large Master Hand assembly conversion into many numbered C files plus a local ftMasterHand.h, which made the large decompilation easier to build, review, and organize than a single giant C file.
Preserved address comments and decomp.me scratch links above many functions, making individual matches traceable back to original addresses and reviewable scratch attempts.
Kept unresolved or low-confidence symbols as address-style names such as lbl_80154230 and func_801545A0 rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.

Introduced local inline helpers for matching-sensitive math in ftMasterHand_22.c, including a custom sqrt/frsqrte path used by my_lbvector_Len, showing a willingness to reproduce compiler codegen when library calls would not match.
Updated build integration and object lists in the same PR as the decompilation so the assembly removal was actually wired into the normal build.
Made frank.py a Makefile dependency for frank-fixed object rules, preventing stale output when the fixing tool itself changes.
decomp_lessons: For large character/boss decompilations, create a dedicated folder early; reviewer PsiLupan explicitly requested that the files be placed in an ftMasterHand folder.

Do not leave temporary build/debug toggles enabled at merge time; a reviewer called out a Makefile change from `GENERATE_MAP ?= 0` to `GENERATE_MAP ?= 1` with 'don't forget to disable this before merging'.
Empty callback functions should be represented cleanly with `return;` when that is the project-preferred style; multiple review comments repeated this for ftMasterHand_25.c through ftMasterHand_30.c.

Matching-sensitive conditions should still be cleaned up when possible; review requested simplifying a multiline decrement/test into `if (--r31_ft_userdata->x2364 <= 0.0f && r31_ft_userdata->x2200_ftcmd_var0)`.

When decompiling fighter code with many state variables, header/struct work is part of the match: the PR added/adjusted Fighter fields for Master Hand state storage rather than hiding everything behind raw offsets.
Address comments and scratch links are useful evidence for future workers, but the PR still left many functions with lbl_/func_ names, so semantic naming remained future work.
naming_conventions: Character folder used CamelCase path `src/melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/` and header `ftMasterHand.h`.
C split files followed numbered names `ftMasterHand_1.c` through `ftMasterHand_34.c`.
Unidentified functions retained project-standard address-derived names such as `lbl_80154230`, `lbl_8015442C`, and `func_801545A0`.
Master Hand-specific attribute type appeared as `MasterHandAttributes`.
Temporary decompiler-style register variable names appeared in the excerpt, such as `r31_ft_userdata`, `r30_attributes`, and `temp_r4`; review did not provide evidence of a broader rename pass.
Fighter struct fields used offset-prefixed names with partial semantics, e.g. `x2344_callback`, `x2348_stateVar3`, `x234C_pos`, `x2358_stateVar7`, `x2200_ftcmd_var0`, and `x80_self_vel`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a monolithic assembly source with many C files while keeping original address comments for function alignment.
Used decomp.me scratch links beside individual functions to document matching attempts and provide reproducible context.
Used a custom inline `my_sqrtf` based on `__frsqrte` Newton iterations plus volatile float storage to match PowerPC floating-point codegen for vector length calculation.
Used explicit state/action IDs such as `0x175`, `0x176`, and `0x17D` in `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC` calls where semantic enum names were not established in the excerpt.
Preserved some fakematch artifacts, such as `s32 unk = 0; // fakematch` and unused stack arrays, when needed for matching.
Adjusted the frank object-fixing Makefile rule to pass exactly the vanilla object, profile object, and output object despite adding $(FRANK) as an additional prerequisite.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Go ahead and put these in a ftMasterHand folder when you get the chance, since I'll ask for that later.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Don't forget to disable the temporary `GENERATE_MAP ?= 1` change before merging.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Fix spacing and simplify the countdown condition to `if (--r31_ft_userdata->x2364 <= 0.0f && r31_ft_userdata->x2200_ftcmd_var0)`.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftMasterHand lbl_/func_ symbols to semantic names.
Search for additional MasterHandAttributes layout refinements after PR #341.
Look for follow-up PRs modifying ftMasterHand_22.c custom sqrt/vector-length matching code.
Check whether the temporary Makefile `GENERATE_MAP` change was reverted before merge in the final diff.
Search for later cleanup of empty Master Hand callbacks to confirm the repeated `return;` review feedback was applied project-wide.
